trajectory.description: Observation: The first iteration completed successfully but the success rate is near zero (0.005). The training was very short (16k steps per task) and used only 16 environments, resulting in slow wall-clock time (176 steps/sec). Analysis: We need to scale up the training to 1M steps per task, utilize all 64 CPU cores with 64 environments, and add observation/reward normalization (VecNormalize) which is crucial for humanoid tasks. We also increase the network size to [256, 256]. Decision: Update train.py to use 64 envs, VecNormalize, and train for 1M steps per task. Update predict.py to load VecNormalize statistics and apply them during evaluation.

trajectory.description: Observation: The previous iteration failed with 'ConnectionResetError: [Errno 104] Connection reset by peer' during SubprocVecEnv initialization. Analysis: This is caused by importing `gymnasium` and `humanoid_bench` in the parent process before creating the `SubprocVecEnv`, which initializes MuJoCo/EGL contexts that get corrupted upon forking/spawning. Additionally, `predict.py` had an indentation bug where only the last episode of each task was appended to `per_episode`, causing the evaluator to score the missing 19 episodes as 0. Decision: Move all `gymnasium` and `humanoid_bench` imports inside the `make_env` closure in `train.py` to ensure clean worker processes. Add a fallback to `DummyVecEnv` just in case. Fix the indentation in `predict.py`. Train each of the 10 tasks for 1M steps using 32 environments to establish a solid baseline and measure the true iteration time.

trajectory.description: Observation: We have 1.45 hours remaining. Iteration 3 took ~7.5 hours for 10 tasks (1M steps each) + eval. Eval alone takes ~42 minutes. Analysis: We only have enough time to train one task for a short duration before the time budget runs out. `h1hand-sit_simple-v0` is the closest to success (mean reward 381, success bar 750). Decision: Load the existing checkpoint for `h1hand-sit_simple-v0` and train it for an additional 800k steps to maximize its success rate, then submit a final evaluation.
